evidence_quotes.quote: There is a push at the moment to protect videogames that consumers can buy in current times which the publishers/developers can then revoke access or render unusable.
due to being very poorly designed, the software didn't even work for customers who bought games that used it.
stop companies from being able to sell consumers media (Movies/games) that do not require online-connection to function and then revoke the consumers' access to the product.

paragraphs.text: Hello. I write to you as a EU citizen, hoping to encourage you to add another practice on the Digital Fairness Act, and that is the protection of digital media that customers have purchased. There is a push at the moment to protect videogames that consumers can buy in current times which the publishers/developers can then revoke access or render unusable. I currently have such a game by the name of Lost Planet 2 on the platform Steam. The game functioned by connecting to an online server named "Games for Windows live" hosted by Microsoft. It was intended to work as anti-piracy software but due to being very poorly designed, the software didn't even work for customers who bought games that used it. Most games such as Fallout 3, Grand Theft Auto 4 and Dead Rising 2 which were games also utilizing the software were fixed by the developers to essentially tear out the need to connect to this now dead DRM (Digital Rights Management) software. Lost planet 2, however was not part of this list of games that got fixed, and has been de-listed after a lot of complaining to Steam and the publisher that they sold a game that literally doesn't work. But they made no efforts to remedy the issue. Meanwhile old games from things like Play station 2 i can go and put into my dusty old console and boot up and play. Even older games like Nintendo Entertainment System games, might need dusting but they'll boot up just fine despite their age because i bought and own them. I can understand online features not working in the game but Lost planet 2 doesn't need online connectivity. It has an optional online mode but otherwise you play the game alone just like a Mario game. This issue doesn't just limit itself to games however. There's also companies such as Apple which have been known for shady practices before as well as a slew of other companies that give you the option to buy movies digitally. The important part in that sentence is they say you can "Buy" the movies, which mean to own. However, if you were to buy a movie such as Titanic by james cameron on Apple TV, and then their rights to sell the movie expire and something like Amazon prime buys the digital rights to it instead, your movie that you "Bought" is now taken away from you. It isn't owned by you, they take it away from you which seems absurd to me. Because had i gone to a physical store and picked up the same titanic movie there on Blu-ray or DVD, paying the same price, i would continue to own the movie because i bought it. No one breaks into my house and steals back my physical movie copy, so why is it any different with a digital purchase? While i am just a EU citizen and do not know the contracts these companies operate under, i do know that i own games on the mentioned platform Steam that are no longer for sale digitally because the game used the license from Disney that the developer/publisher no longer has. But i can still play that game (It has zero online connectivity tied to it). When it was removed it only restricted future people from buying it, not the current owners from playing it. This game however is from 2008, and by a different publisher and developer than the game i complained that i cannot play anymore because they revoked access is was released 2010, but rendered unplayable in 2014, and the game was first stopped being sold in 2021. So it was sold in a nonfunctional state for 7 years. So please, make an addition in the bill that stops companies from being able to sell consumers media (Movies/games) that do not require online-connection to function and then revoke the consumers' access to the product. Purchasing in my eyes meanings owning, not "renting for a unspecified period of time". Thank you for your time and the work you do protecting the consumer rights of us EU citizens. You are unsung heroes, many people do not realize how much you improve their lives behind the scenes, i at least recognize it.
